fresh_facts.claim: The US deployed approximately 2,200-2,500 Marines from the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit via amphibious ships including the USS Tripoli toward the Middle East and Strait of Hormuz.
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth approved the Marine deployment at Central Command's request, with the unit positioned over a week away from the region.
US and Israeli forces conducted strikes on Iranian military targets on Kharg Island using 5,000-pound GPS-guided bombs.
fresh_facts.source: US Central Command / Trump social media

fresh_facts.claim: Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib was killed in an overnight strike in Tehran as of March 18, 2026.
Iran's security chief Ali Larijani and Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani were killed in an Israeli strike on Tuesday, March 17, 2026.
Mojtaba Khamenei, son of former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei, has been introduced as the new leader of Iran's regime.
Mojtaba Khamenei's first official message emphasized continuation of the war effort and threatened to close the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing conflict.
Israeli officials reported killing two senior intelligence figures linked to Khatam al-Anbiya emergency command: Abdollah Jalali-Nasab and Amir Shariat.
Drone strikes targeted Basij and IRGC-linked checkpoints across multiple Iranian cities with rising casualties among forces deployed to control movement.
Residents in Tehran neighborhoods including Saadat Abad reported anti-IRGC night chants including 'death to the IRGC' despite expanded regime patrols and checkpoints.
The UN Fact-Finding Mission on Iran warned that civilians are trapped between armed conflict and repression at levels that may amount to crimes against humanity.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi denied that Iran targeted civilian areas in neighboring states and proposed a joint investigative committee with regional governments.
Hamas publicly urged the Iranian regime not to target neighboring Arab countries, marking a break from Tehran's position.
Albania's Prime Minister Edi Rama announced plans to invite parliament to vote on declaring Iran a state sponsor of terrorism, with the vote scheduled for the week of March 13.
European sanctions on Iran were snapped back as part of continued US maximum pressure campaign through sanctions.
Iran sustained air campaigns and missile/drone attacks on Persian Gulf shipping and energy infrastructure, driving oil prices higher.
A large explosion hit a Tehran square during Quds Day protests with reported chants of death to Israel and death to America.
President Trump announced that US and Israeli strikes spared Iranian oil infrastructure on Kharg Island but warned of future action if Hormuz shipping is disrupted.
Vice President Vance stated uncertainty on whether US or Israel injured Iran's new Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei.
President Trump noted the Basij paramilitary's role in suppressing protests with machine guns in a Friday Fox News Radio interview.

regime_fall.current_status: The Iranian regime faces accelerating military degradation and internal destabilization following the succession of Mojtaba Khamenei, who has signaled continuation of confrontation while the regime loses senior command figures, experiences public anti-IRGC sentiment despite enhanced security measures, and confronts expanding US military deployment (2,200-2,500 Marines en route) combined with coordinated strikes targeting military and economic infrastructure including Kharg Island.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US and Israeli forces conducted comprehensive strikes on March 17-19 targeting Iranian military command structures, killing senior officials including Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib, Basij Chief Gholamreza Soleimani, Security Chief Ali Larijani, and intelligence figures Abdollah Jalali-Nasab and Amir Shariat, while striking Kharg Island with 5,000-pound bombs and positioning 2,200-2,500 Marines for imminent regional deployment, as Mojtaba Khamenei assumed leadership with threats to close the Strait of Hormuz.
